core_mechanism: direct pointwise recovery of an unknown distribution as its overlap (inner-product) integral against a tunable/displaceable reference distribution, so a single direct measurement yields the function value without solving an inverse (tomographic) reconstruction problem
brief_reason: The abstract measures the overlap of two Wigner (quasiprobability) functions via photon counting and thereby obtains the phase-space distribution directly, avoiding tomographic reconstruction algorithms — i.e., sampling a function through its inner product with a reference.
